citations.narrative:
The facility has been conducting regulated activity without a valid USDA license. The owner was contacted by USDA
officials in August 2025 and was informed that a license was required to exhibit their current animals on hand. Due to a
complaint, a USDA official inspected this facility and did determine the facility is still conducting regulated activity by
exhibiting birds, guinea pigs, ferrets, goats, sheep, a hedgehog, llama, and zebu. A valid license is necessary to protect
the health and well-being of the animals and to comply with the Animal Welfare Act and Regulations. The facility needs to
obtain a USDA license or discontinue regulated activities.
No regulated activities may be conducted until a USDA license is obtained.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representative.
n

narrative:
2.1(a)(1) 
   
Requirements and application. 
The facility has been conducting regulated activity without a valid USDA license. The owner was contacted by USDA 
officials in August 2025 and was informed that a license was required to exhibit their current animals on hand. Due to a 
complaint, a USDA official inspected this facility and did determine the facility is still conducting regulated activity by 
exhibiting birds, guinea pigs, ferrets, goats, sheep, a hedgehog, llama, and zebu. A valid license is necessary to protect 
the health and well-being of the animals and to comply with the Animal Welfare Act and Regulations. The facility needs to 
obtain a USDA license or discontinue regulated activities. 
 
No regulated activities may be conducted until a USDA license is obtained. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representative.
